title: Default and Direct Exchanges, Work Queues, and Fair Dispatch · Type-System Trace: Worker Effect Evidence
summary: A task service publishes through the default exchange without naming the coupling, assumes one queue broadcasts, uses auto-ack workers, and calls round-robin dispatch fair despite unequal task cost. Planned artifact: Add and verify the worker effect evidence increment in direct-routing work distribution service. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Acknowledge only after a durable effect or safely repeatable handoff and retain message identity, attempt, result, and failure evidence.
Measure service time, prefetch, unacknowledged work, capacity, and tenant fairness instead of equating round robin with fair completion.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Predict Worker Effect Evidence · Theory Worker Effect Evidence
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory messaging team handles case 8215 in a recovery drill with stale callbacks from an old channel generation. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to combine direct routing, worker capacity, and acknowledgement-after-effect evidence; allow exactly one terminal acknowledgement decision owner; then make the broker confirmation disappear after the message may have committed. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Worker Effect Evidence contract for the supplied data.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Build Worker Effect Evidence · Theory Worker Effect Evidence
